root_cause: The join-request rumor callback registered in startWelcomeSubscription (app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx, formerly lines 811-815) only called incrementJoinRequest(request.groupId) — the reactive bell-counter store — and relied on joinRequestHandler.ts having already persisted the PendingJoinRequest to IDB. It never touched the pendingRequests React state (declared at MarmotContext.tsx:381), which is written ONLY by loadPendingRequestsForGroup (MarmotContext.tsx:1415-1419), a one-shot IDB read invoked ONLY by PendingRequestsSection.tsx's mount-only useEffect([groupId]) (lines 82-84). An admin who stayed on an already-mounted group detail page therefore never got a re-read: the bell updated live (separate store, separate rendering path) but the pending-requests section stayed stale until navigate-away/reload remounted it. The working invite-by-npub/commit path was unaffected because it updates the shared `groups` state via onMembersChanged, which does propagate live to mounted consumers.

fix.description: Added a setPendingRequests state append inside the existing join-request callback, mirroring the exact pattern already used by approveJoinRequest/denyJoinRequest (MarmotContext.tsx:1434-1437, 1449-1452). The append is idempotent by eventId dedup, so it converges correctly under both possible event orderings: if PendingRequestsSection's mount effect runs after the live callback, the append is redundant but harmless (loadPendingRequestsForGroup performs a full IDB-authoritative replace); if the live callback runs after mount (the bug's actual scenario), the append is what makes the already-mounted section update. No other function, listener, or dispatcher was touched.

files_changed.change: join-request callback now calls setPendingRequests((prev) => ...) with eventId-deduped append, alongside the pre-existing incrementJoinRequest call

tests_added.description: New groups/relay-bucket e2e spec (3 tests, test.describe.serial). Admin (User A) creates a group, opens the detail view, generates+copies an invite link, closes the modal, and STAYS on the open group-detail page for the remainder of the test (no goto/reload). Invitee (User B) opens the invite link in a second browser context and sends a join request through the app. The final test asserts, on User A's still-open page: (1) the notification bell badge becomes visible via expect.poll (contrast — proves the event was received live), then (2) the pending-requests-section becomes visible via a plain toBeVisible with no navigation (the assertion that fails pre-fix and passes post-fix), plus a concrete pending-request-row is visible.

e2e_new_spec_pre_fix.failing_test: User A sees both the live bell update AND the live pending-requests section, without navigating away
e2e_new_spec_pre_fix.failure_mode: pending-requests-section toBeVisible timed out at 30s (bell poll passed within the first ~3s interval, confirming the event WAS received live — the correct pre-fix failure signature)

e2e_new_spec_post_fix.third_test_duration: 3.0s (down from 33.1s pre-fix, confirming the section now appears immediately rather than timing out)

e2e_regression_groups_lazy_updates.note: One flaky-looking failure was observed on an EARLIER run of this spec, but only when the relay had NOT been wiped between three prior spec-file runs in the same docker session. Verified by (a) stashing the fix and re-running against the same unwiped relay — test 3 PASSED without the fix present too, and (b) re-running WITH the fix against a freshly wiped relay — all 6 passed. This isolates the earlier failure to accumulated strfry/KeyPackage state across unwiped runs (the exact contamination the project's own `make test-e2e` target wipes volumes to avoid before every full-suite run), not to this fix.

verification_questions.Q1-REPRO: YES. app/tests/e2e/groups-join-request-live.spec.ts keeps User A on the open group-detail page for the entire test — no pageA.goto or pageA.reload appears anywhere after 'User A creates a group and generates an invite link'. Confirmed pre-fix: 2 passed / 1 failed, with the failure being exactly the pending-requests-section timeout (bell poll succeeded first). Confirmed post-fix: 3/3 passed, third test dropping from 33.1s to 3.0s.
verification_questions.Q2-ROOTCAUSE: YES. Root cause is precisely that the join-request callback (MarmotContext.tsx startWelcomeSubscription 5th arg) updated only incrementJoinRequest (bell store) and relied on IDB persistence done inside joinRequestHandler.ts, but never called setPendingRequests — the React state PendingRequestsSection actually renders from, which is otherwise refreshed only by a mount-only useEffect.
verification_questions.Q3-BELL-CONTRAST: YES. The final test explicitly polls for the bell badge first (expect.poll, ~60s budget) before asserting on pending-requests-section. Pre-fix, the bell poll passed quickly (well under its interval budget) while the section assertion timed out — this is the evidence that the event WAS received and processed (ruling out 'event never arrived') and that the gap is specifically the missing state propagation into the open view.
verification_questions.Q4-MINIMAL: YES. The fix is a single setPendingRequests append added to the existing callback, textually mirroring the current setPendingRequests filter pattern in approveJoinRequest/denyJoinRequest. No dispatcher change, no new synchronization primitive, no touched files outside MarmotContext.tsx (fix) and the new spec (test). Idempotent via eventId dedup — verified no double-count: groups-invite-link.spec.ts's 'User A sees the pending join request' test (which exercises the mount-effect ordering, arriving via /groups/ navigate-back) still shows exactly one row and 'approve' still empties the section correctly.
verification_questions.Q5-REGRESSION: YES, on a clean relay. groups-invite-link.spec.ts: 6/6 passed. groups-lazy-updates.spec.ts: 6/6 passed on a freshly wiped relay (and separately confirmed the one observed failure on an unwiped relay was pre-existing infra contamination, reproduced identically with the fix stashed out — see test_counts note above). No duplicate/double-counted rows observed in either mount-then-callback or callback-then-mount orderings across both regression specs and the new spec.
verification_questions.Q6-PRIVACY-ARCH: YES. The change touches only client-side React state (setPendingRequests) fed by data that was already being persisted to IDB and already traveled only through the existing NIP-59 gift-wrapped join-request rumor channel (addressed, encrypted, not a public broadcast). No new publish call, no new relay subscription, no new public event kind. Static-export/query-param conventions untouched — no routing change of any kind.

baseline_vs_final.unit_tests: 141 files / 2507 tests passing before and after — no regressions, no new unit tests (bug is e2e-only reproducible: it requires a live cross-context relay event and an already-mounted React component)
baseline_vs_final.typecheck: clean before and after (npx tsc --noEmit, exit 0)
baseline_vs_final.lint: NO_LINT both before and after — no ESLint config present in app/; `next lint` prompts interactively to scaffold one and was not invoked as part of this surgical fix

retrospective.reason: routine — root cause was pre-confirmed with exact file:line references, the fix shape was fully specified, and implementation matched the spec on the first attempt with no debugging cycles. The only friction (an apparent regression in groups-lazy-updates.spec.ts) traced cleanly to pre-existing relay-volume contamination between back-to-back unwiped test runs — an artifact of my own verification sequencing, not a gap in the spec or a durable technical insight worth recording.
